title: FHP correspondence with Stanford Open Policing Project, 2014–2019

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: FHP correspondence with Stanford Open Policing Project, 2014–2019
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

All correspondence (including emails, letters, and meeting notes) between FHP or FLHSMV staff and the Stanford Open Policing Project — including but not limited to Sharad Goel, William Cai, or any other Stanford University researcher — between January 1, 2014 and December 31, 2019, concerning the transmission of FHP citation data, including any record that discusses the inclusion or exclusion of an Ethnicity field in any dataset shared with Stanford.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

John Cranston

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: FHP General Orders, 2016–2018
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

All FHP General Orders issued or amended between January 1, 2016 and December 31, 2018 that pertain to traffic-stop reporting, citation completion, racial or ethnic classification of drivers, or eCitation procedures — including, but not limited to, General Orders 2.02, 6.02, and 17.06 in effect during that period.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

John Cranston

title: Public Records Request — FHP race/ethnicity field documentation, 2014–2019

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Public Records Request — FHP race/ethnicity field documentation, 2014–2019
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any user-interface screenshots, screen-design documents, field-level data dictionaries, training materials, or user guides that show or describe how race and ethnicity are entered into FHP’s eCitation, CAD, or RMS software at any time between January 1, 2014 and December 31, 2019.

For any of the above records held by Caliber Public Safety on FHP’s behalf, please obtain them under Section 119.0701, Florida Statutes.

I am an academic researcher and the records will be used for non-commercial educational research published in the public interest. I respectfully request a waiver or reduction of any fees under Section 119.07(1), Florida Statutes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

John Cranston

title: Public Records Request — FHP citation software deployment dates, 2014–2019

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Public Records Request — FHP citation software deployment dates, 2014–2019
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

1.	The deployment date (or “go-live” date) at each FHP station or troop for each version of any Caliber Public Safety, SmartCOP, or CTS America software product (including CAD NG, eCitation, RMS, Field-Based Reporting, and Mobile modules) that was used by FHP between January 1, 2014 and December 31, 2019.
2.	All release notes, change logs, or version-history documents that Caliber Public Safety, SmartCOP, or CTS America provided to FHP between January 1, 2014 and December 31, 2019.

For any of the above records held by Caliber Public Safety on FHP’s behalf, please obtain them under Section 119.0701, Florida Statutes.
I am an academic researcher and the records will be used for non-commercial educational research published in the public interest. I respectfully request a waiver or reduction of any fees under Section 119.07(1), Florida Statutes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

John Cranston
